description_long: This object consists of four distinct wooden chairs arranged side by side, each featuring a different seat and backrest upholstery pattern. The chairs have straight backs and square seats, with visible wooden frames and four sturdy legs. The upholstery varies: one chair has a plaid fabric, another has a blue textured fabric, a third has a beige woven pattern, and the last is covered in a gray fabric. The wood finishes also differ, ranging from natural brown to painted black and white. The overall design is simple and functional, suitable for communal or public seating in urban environments. The chairs appear robust and are likely intended for regular use in settings such as parks, waiting areas, or outdoor cafes.
description: A set of four wooden chairs with varied upholstery patterns and finishes, featuring straight backs and square seats. Each chair has a unique combination of wood color and fabric design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the profile of all four chairs lined up, highlighting the thickness of the seats and backrests.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the distinct seat and backrest patterns and colors of each chair, with all four chairs visible in a row.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the side profiles and the alignment of the chairs' legs and seats.
description_view_3: Rear view revealing the backrest designs and wood finishes, with the fabric patterns less visible.
